title: Applications of Object Detection
concept: Object detection is used across industries to enable machines to reason about the positions and identities of multiple physical objects.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main difference between single-object and multi-object detection?

options.text: Multi-object models handle a variable number of objects, while single-object assumes exactly one
Single-object models are only used for text classification
Multi-object models only output class labels

exercises.explain: Multi-object detection handles a dynamic and unknown number of objects in the frame.

cards.title: Robo-sorting Warehouse
cards.body: In a modern fulfillment center, robotic arms sort thousands of items per hour. They can't just know 'there is a box' on the conveyor belt — they need to know its exact coordinates and orientation to pick it up without dropping it.

cards.title: Industrial Applications
cards.body: Object Detection Applications span autonomous driving, medical imaging, security, agriculture, and manufacturing, allowing computers to interact spatially with the physical world.

cards.title: Eyes for the Machine
cards.body: Just as human eyes allow you to reach out and grab a cup of coffee without knocking it over, object detection provides the spatial coordinates that robots and software need to interact with objects.

cards.title: Tumor Detection
cards.body: Radiologists use object detection to scan MRI images. The model automatically highlights potential tumors with bounding boxes, helping doctors identify anomalies faster.

cards.title: Not just for static images
cards.body: It's easy to think object detection is only for photos. In reality, it runs on video feeds in real-time (by processing frames sequentially), enabling live security monitoring and self-driving navigation.

cards.title: Practical Use Cases
cards.body: Comparing static classification vs spatial detection uses:

exercises.prompt: Which of the following applications strictly requires object detection rather than simple classification?
exercises.explain: Counting individual objects requires locating each one in the image, which is an object detection task.

options.text: Counting the number of apples on a tree branch
Determining if a photo was taken during the day or night
Filtering out spam emails based on subject lines
Predicting tomorrow's temperature in degrees Fahrenheit

exercises.prompt: True or False: Real-time video object detection is accomplished by running the detector on individual video frames over time.
exercises.explain: True. Videos are sequences of images, so object detection models process individual frames to detect moving objects.
Object detection helps medical staff locate anomalies, such as tumors or fractures, by boxing them.
exercises.sentence: Medical imaging uses object detection to draw bounding boxes around potential ___ in scans.

exercises.prompt: Which system is using object detection?
exercises.explain: Highlighting face locations with bounding boxes is a classic application of object detection.

options.text: A filter that turns photos into oil paintings
A camera app that highlights faces with yellow boxes for auto-focus
A program that compresses files to save space

exercises.prompt: You are building a drone system to inspect solar panels in a massive desert field. The drone needs to locate cracked solar panels so technicians can find and replace them easily. What system do you deploy on the drone?

options.text: A classifier that outputs 'damaged' or 'intact' for the whole field view.
An object detector that outputs coordinates of each individual damaged panel relative to the drone's GPS path.
A standard CNN with high feature density to compress the image sizes.
A regression model to predict the total number of solar panels.

exercises.explain: Exactly! The technicians need the specific coordinates to find the panels, making object detection the right tool. Next: let's learn how neural networks process images to detect objects!
